articles.title: Crash Closes Heywood Old Road in Middleton
articles.excerpt: Emergency crews are responding to a crash on Heywood Old Road in Middleton, which has resulted in the road being closed. Drivers are advised to avoid the area.
articles.content_html:
A crash has occurred on Heywood Old Road in Middleton, prompting emergency crews to respond to the scene. The road is currently closed due to the incident.

Motorists are advised to avoid the area while crews manage the situation. No further details about the crash have been released.

Authorities have not provided information regarding any injuries or the cause of the crash. Updates will follow as more information becomes available.

Anyone with information can find Greater Manchester Police and Crimestoppers contact details at the end of this article.

articles.title: Two people die in crash on Highway 101 near Kingston
articles.excerpt: A 75-year-old woman and a 28-year-old man, both from Middleton, died following a collision involving a tractor-trailer and a small car on Highway 101.
articles.content_html:
A 75-year-old woman and a 28-year-old man, both from Middleton, died at the scene following a crash on Highway 101 near Kingston on Tuesday afternoon.

Police reported that a tractor-trailer hauling hay collided with a small car at the Kingston exit. The impact resulted in the deaths of both occupants of the car.

Emergency services attended the scene, but both were pronounced dead shortly after the incident. The identities of the deceased have not yet been released pending notification of their families.

An investigation into the circumstances of the crash is ongoing. Anyone with information can find Greater Manchester Police and Crimestoppers contact details at the end of this article.

articles.title: Potholes on Rochdale-Oldham Border Cause Frustration for Local Councillor
articles.excerpt: Residents have raised concerns over potholes at a junction on the border of Rochdale and Oldham, leading to frustration from local councillor Dylan Williams.
articles.content_html:
Potholes at a major junction on the border of Oldham and Rochdale have prompted complaints from residents. Councillor Dylan Williams, who represents East Middleton at Rochdale town hall, has expressed frustration over the lack of action regarding the deteriorating road surfaces between the two boroughs.

The issue is located at the junction where Greengate meets Grimshaw Lane, near a 'Welcome to Rochdale' sign, on the route into Middleton. Councillor Williams stated that despite being responsible for highways in Rochdale, he cannot address the potholes as they are situated just over the border in Oldham.

Councillor Williams noted that the potholes were marked for repair in March but have not been addressed since. He described the impact on vehicles, saying, "The bus rattles like mad when they go over it and so do cars." He added that with Oldham council currently lacking a leader, there is no clear point of contact for complaints other than council officers.

In response, Oldham council acknowledged the concerns regarding the potholes and confirmed that their highways team has inspected the area. They stated that the site is being considered for future planned repair works, subject to priorities across the borough.
articles.legal_disclaimer: This article was compiled from identified public sources and may be updated when further verified information becomes available.

articles.title: Car Crashes into Restaurant in Rochdale, Causing Significant Damage
articles.excerpt: A car crashed into the front of the Amor restaurant in Bamford, Rochdale, on Monday, leaving the building damaged but with no reported injuries.
articles.content_html:
ONGOING STORY — Updated 15 July 2026 at 06:37. 3 source updates have been combined into this article.

A car crashed into the front of the Amor restaurant in Bamford, Rochdale, on Monday, leaving the building damaged but with no reported injuries.

Latest update

A car crashed into the front of the Amor restaurant in Bamford, Rochdale, on Monday, leaving the building damaged but with no reported injuries.

A car crashed into a restaurant in Rochdale, causing significant damage to the front of the building. The incident occurred on Tuesday morning, leaving the restaurant's exterior shattered.

Emergency services attended the scene, but no injuries have been reported. The driver of the car is believed to have lost control before colliding with the restaurant.

Local authorities are assessing the damage, and the restaurant is expected to remain closed for repairs. The incident has raised concerns about road safety in the area.

Earlier developments

Anyone with information can find Greater Manchester Police and Crimestoppers contact details at the end of this article. Our thoughts are with the family and all those affected.

A car crashed into the front of the Amor restaurant in Bamford, Rochdale, on Monday (July 13), causing significant damage to the building. The incident occurred around midday, prompting an emergency response that included the Greater Manchester Fire and Rescue Service and Greater Manchester Police.

Pictures shared online showed the glass front of the Indian restaurant completely shattered, with fire service tape cordoning off the area following the collision. The restaurant announced it would be closed for the evening due to the incident, stating on social media: "Due to unforeseen circumstances we will be closed tonight."

A spokesperson for Greater Manchester Fire and Rescue Service confirmed that fire crews were called to the scene just after 12.15pm. One fire engine from Rochdale station attended the incident, which involved a car crashing into the commercial building. Firefighters worked to make the area safe before handing the scene over to police. They remained on site for approximately an hour.

There were no reports of injuries following the crash. Greater Manchester Police were also approached for a statement regarding the incident.

A car crashed into a restaurant in Rochdale on 15 July 2026, resulting in significant damage to the front of the building. Specific details about the time of the incident were not provided.

Emergency services responded to the scene, and no injuries have been reported. The extent of the damage suggests that repairs will be necessary before the restaurant can reopen.

Witnesses described the impact as loud, with debris scattered across the area. Local authorities are expected to assess the damage and determine the next steps for the property.

Update timeline

  • 15 July 2026, 06:37: Car Crashes into Restaurant in Rochdale, Causing Significant Damage
  • 13 July 2026, 21:00: Car crashes into restaurant in Bamford, causing significant damage

articles.title: Rochdale's Sudden Junction Traffic Delays Under Scrutiny Amid Roadworks
articles.excerpt: Roadworks at Sudden Junction in Rochdale have led to significant traffic delays, prompting a personal commute challenge to assess the situation.
articles.content_html:
Roadworks at Sudden Junction in Rochdale have caused significant traffic delays since they began in May. The junction connects major roads from Heywood and Castleton with Roch Valley Way and the Rochdale bypass, leading to complaints from local drivers about congestion during peak times.

A local crossing guard warned that it could take more than 15 minutes to navigate the area. A recent personal assessment of the route confirmed this estimate, with a journey from Bolton Road to the bypass taking approximately 15 minutes, despite the roadworks. The assessment began on Bolton Road, where the crossing guard's prediction proved accurate, although it was noted that this route is not a primary commuter path.

The ongoing construction aims to implement an 'intelligent signal system' designed to alleviate traffic congestion. Observers noted that the work included resurfacing the road and creating a new traffic light system, which is expected to improve flow in the long term. A massive pneumatic drill was heard working on the site, indicating active progress on the project. The local authority has stated that traffic problems have eased at peak times in recent weeks due to their interventions.

As the roadworks continue, local residents are likely to experience ongoing delays, particularly during rush hour. The complexities of the junction, likened to Birmingham's Spaghetti Junction, have made it a focal point for traffic issues in the area. The improvements being made are intended to address these long-standing concerns.

articles.title: Teen Faces Charges After High-Speed Chase in Middleton
articles.excerpt: An 18-year-old man is facing charges following a high-speed police chase in Middleton that reached speeds of 130 miles per hour and ended in a crash.
articles.content_html:
An 18-year-old man is facing charges after police say he led officers on a high-speed chase in Middleton. The pursuit reportedly reached speeds of 130 miles per hour before ending in a crash.

The incident began when officers attempted to stop the man for a traffic violation. He allegedly failed to stop and accelerated, prompting a chase through the area.

The chase concluded with a collision, although no injuries have been reported. Police have not disclosed further details regarding the charges he faces.

Anyone with information can find Greater Manchester Police and Crimestoppers contact details at the end of this article.

articles.title: Traffic Incidents Reported in Rochdale
articles.excerpt: A series of traffic incidents are currently affecting travel in Rochdale, with drivers facing delays due to accidents and adverse weather conditions.
articles.content_html:
Rochdale is experiencing a series of traffic incidents that are significantly disrupting travel across the area. As of the latest reports, drivers are urged to exercise caution and consider potential delays when planning their journeys. The ongoing disruptions stem from a combination of accidents and adverse weather conditions, which have collectively contributed to increased congestion on local roads.

In recent weeks, there has been a notable rise in traffic incidents within Rochdale, raising concerns among commuters and residents alike. The local council has faced calls to address these issues, particularly during peak travel times when congestion tends to peak. The impact of these incidents is particularly pronounced for those who rely on road transport for their daily commutes, as any disruption can lead to longer travel times and heightened frustration.

For families and businesses in the area, the implications of these traffic disruptions are considerable. Parents may find it challenging to drop off children at schools on time, while local businesses could experience delays in deliveries and customer access. The cumulative effect of these incidents may lead to a ripple of inconvenience throughout the community, affecting not only individual journeys but also the overall flow of daily life in Rochdale.

Drivers are encouraged to consult reliable sources for the latest traffic conditions. Platforms such as Traffic Update Rochdale provide live reports on incidents, weather conditions, and nearby speed cameras, which can assist in navigating the current disruptions. By utilising these resources, motorists may be able to avoid the worst of the delays and plan alternative routes if necessary.

As the situation continues to evolve, residents are invited to report any new incidents or hazards they encounter on the roads. This proactive approach can help ensure that the community remains informed and safe during these challenging conditions, fostering a collective effort to mitigate the impact of ongoing traffic disruptions.
